title: Fluffy Thai Mushroom Tom Yum
description: This ovo-vegetarian friendly Tom Yum dish features robata-grilled mushrooms and a rich, umami broth, with a unique touch of celery salt, making it a festive and delicious Central Thai inspired meal.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to medium high heat.
In a small bowl, mix together tom yum paste and vegetable oil to make a marinade.
Add the sliced onion to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Grill the onion slices for 2-3 minutes per side, until they are lightly charred.
In a large pot, combine the grilled onion, lemongrass, garlic, and vegetable broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the mushrooms to the pot and cook until they are tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk and celery salt, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, temper the beaten eggs by slowly adding a little of the hot broth to the eggs, whisking constantly.
Add the tempered eggs to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, until the eggs are cooked through and the soup is fluffy.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the Tom Yum hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
